description: R package for behavioral economic demand analysis. Fits exponential demand curves, mixed-effects models, and hurdle models for purchase task data.
rules: All modeling functions expect long-format data with columns: id (subject), x (price), y (consumption).
Prefer modern API (fit_demand_fixed, fit_demand_mixed, fit_demand_hurdle) over legacy FitCurves.
For zero-heavy data, use fit_demand_hurdle() or use simplified exponential in fit_demand_mixed().
Parameter names: Q0 = intensity, alpha = elasticity, k = range constant, Pmax = price at max expenditure.
Use get_subject_pars(), extract_coefficients(), or coef() to extract fitted parameters.
Cross-price functions are fit_cp_nls(), fit_cp_linear(), fit_cp_hurdle() — not demand functions.
Prefer pkgdown vignettes for workflow guidance: beezdemand, model-selection, hurdle-demand-models.
Dev docs at https://brentkaplan.github.io/beezdemand/dev/ for in-progress features.
Do not assume undocumented functions exist — verify against the reference index.
